## Session 2026-07-20 — task-001: Config refactor

### Completed
- Added `_runtimeEndpoint` module-level variable, `setRuntimeEndpoint()` with strict validation (sandbox/production/null, throws on invalid)
- Added `resolveEndpoint()` with priority chain: runtime override > NODE_ENV > stored config > 'production' default
- Refactored `apiBaseUrl()` and `authBaseUrl()` to use `resolveEndpoint()` instead of old `isDev()`
- Updated `isDev()` to delegate to `resolveEndpoint() === 'sandbox'` for backward compatibility
- Exported `setRuntimeEndpoint` and `resolveEndpoint` alongside existing exports
- All 70 existing tests pass, including config.test.js save/load/clear round-trip tests
- Smoke-tested all acceptance criteria manually

### Key Decisions
- `resolveEndpoint()` calls `load()` each time to read stored config — simple and correct, no caching layer needed at this stage
- Env vars (`MAYAR_API_URL`/`MAYAR_AUTH_URL`) remain absolute precedence in their respective URL functions

### What Next
- `api.js` still snapshots `BASE_URL` at module load time — will need dynamic resolution per plan Phase 1
- Login and init commands need `--sandbox`/`--production`/`--env` flag handling to call `setRuntimeEndpoint()`

---

## Session 2026-07-20 — task-002: Dynamic base URL + flag parsing

### Completed
- Removed module-level `const BASE_URL` from `api.js`; `request()` now calls `config.apiBaseUrl()` on every invocation, resolving the environment dynamically
- Removed `BASE_URL` from module exports (was only used internally and in tests)
- Added `--sandbox` and `--production` boolean flags to `parseFlags()`
- Added `--env <value>` explicit flag handler to `parseFlags()` (generic `--key=value` handler already catches `--env=value` inline form)
- Added endpoint resolution block in `run()`: validates mutual exclusivity of sandbox/production, validates --env values, calls `config.setRuntimeEndpoint()` with the resolved value or null
- Changed `ensureKey()` auto-prompt to use `config.resolveEndpoint()` instead of hardcoded `'production'` so it respects flag/saved endpoint
- Updated HELP text: added `--sandbox`, `--production`, `--env` to Global flags; revised Environment section with new resolution order
- All 69 existing tests pass (cli, config, ui, util) — no regressions

### Key Decisions
- `--env=sandbox` (inline form) is handled by the existing generic `--key=value` handler, no explicit code needed
- `--env` without `=` is handled explicitly to ensure the next arg is consumed as the value
- Endpoint resolution happens *before* other run logic (version, help, command dispatch) so the endpoint is set for all commands
- Case-insensitive validation for `--env` values (`.toLowerCase()`) for user-friendliness

---

## Session 2026-07-20 — task-003: Interactive environment selection for mayar login

### Completed
- Added interactive TTY prompt in `src/commands/login.js` after `ui.printBanner()`:
  - Only triggers when: not `--json`, no `--sandbox`/`--production`/`--env` flags, `stdin.isTTY`, no stored `endpoint` in config, and `resolveEndpoint()` returns 'production' (i.e., NODE_ENV isn't 'development')
  - Shows "Select environment" with [1] Production / [2] Sandbox options
  - Uses `ui.ask('Choose [1]: ')` with default to '1' (production) on empty input
  - Maps '2'/'sandbox' → sandbox, everything else → production
  - Calls `config.setRuntimeEndpoint()` with the chosen value
- Moved `authUrl` computation to after the prompt so `MayarAuth` SDK gets the correct URL
- Added endpoint display line: `Endpoint: production|sandbox` after the auth server line
- Added `endpoint: config.resolveEndpoint()` to the top-level of saved config alongside `auth`
- Added `endpoint` field to JSON output (`ui.jsonOut()`)
- All 70 existing tests pass — no regressions

### Key Decisions
- Prompt only when truly no preference exists: checks flags, TTY, stored endpoint, and NODE_ENV before prompting. A stored `endpoint` (even 'production') suppresses the prompt.
- `setRuntimeEndpoint()` is called inside login.js (even though cli.js already calls it) because the user's selection needs to override the null set by cli.js. The priority chain in config handles this correctly.
- Empty/invalid input defaults to production — safest default for new users.

### What Next
- task-004: Mirror environment selection into `mayar init`
- task-005+: `mayar skill install` command

---

## Session 2026-07-20 — task-004: Add interactive environment selection to mayar init

### Completed
- Added interactive TTY prompt in `src/commands/init.js` after API key check/overwrite logic:
  - Only triggers when: no `--sandbox`/`--production`/`--env` flags AND `stdin.isTTY`
  - Shows "Select environment" with [1] Production / [2] Sandbox options
  - Uses `ui.ask('Choose [1]: ')` with default to '1' (production) on empty input
  - Maps '2'/'sandbox' → sandbox, everything else → production
  - Calls `config.setRuntimeEndpoint()` with the chosen value
- Welcome message URL is now dynamic: `https://web.mayar.club` for sandbox, `https://web.mayar.id` for production
- `config.save()` now uses `config.resolveEndpoint()` instead of hardcoded `'production'`
- Added endpoint display line: `${ui.dim('Endpoint:')} ${endpoint}` after the "Saved" line
- All 70 existing tests pass — no regressions

### Key Decisions
- Unlike login.js, init.js does NOT check for stored endpoint or NODE_ENV before prompting — the task spec only requires checking flags and isTTY. This is appropriate because init is a setup command that should always offer the choice when interactive.
- The prompt placement (after overwrite check, before welcome message) ensures the welcome message URL is correct.
- `config.setRuntimeEndpoint()` is called inside init.js (even though cli.js already calls it) because the user's selection overrides the null set by cli.js.

### What Next
- task-005+: `mayar skill install` command

---

## Session 2026-07-20 — task-005: Create src/commands/skill.js

### Completed
- Created `src/commands/skill.js` with `run(ctx)` export matching project convention
- Parses subcommand from positional[0]; 'install' triggers action, no subcommand/'help' shows usage, unknown errors with exit(1)
- Fetches SKILL.md from `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mayarid/mayar-cli/refs/heads/main/SKILL.md` via `https.get`
- Follows 301/302 redirects up to 3 hops with proper `res.resume()` on redirected responses
- Defines 5 target paths: `.agents/skills/mayar/SKILL.md`, `.claude/skills/mayar/SKILL.md`, `.opencode/skills/mayar/SKILL.md`, `.codex/skills/mayar/SKILL.md`, `.cursor/rules/mayar.mdc`
- `--target` flag (default 'all') filters to specific agent; validates against: all, agents, claude, opencode, codex, cursor
- Creates parent dirs with `fs.mkdirSync({recursive:true})`
- Skips existing files unless `--force` is set; prints skip message mentioning `--force`
- `--json` outputs JSON array of `{target, path, status}` with status 'installed' or 'skipped'
- Uses only Node.js built-ins: `https`, `fs`, `path` + project-internal `ui` module for formatting
- All 70 existing tests continue to pass (no regressions)

### Key Decisions
- Cursor target (`.cursor/rules/mayar.mdc`) is included in the install loop with raw SKILL.md content; task-006 will add `.mdc` frontmatter adaptation
- Redirect-following uses internal `doFetch` closure to avoid recursion pollution; `new URL(res.headers.location, url)` handles relative redirects
- `--target` flag is parsed by cli.js's generic `--key=value` handler (no explicit handler needed in parseFlags)
- Error messages go to stderr with `ui.red()` for consistency; usage/install messages go to stdout

### What Next
- task-006: CLI registration in cli.js (add `skill` to handlers map), HELP text update, Cursor `.mdc` adaptation with YAML frontmatter

---

## Session 2026-07-20 — task-006: Cursor .mdc adaptation

### Completed
- Added `generateCursorMdc(skillContent)` function in `src/commands/skill.js`:
  - Strips original SKILL.md YAML frontmatter by finding `---` on line 0 and closing `---` on a subsequent line, then slicing the body from after that
  - If no frontmatter detected (line 0 isn't `---`), preserves raw content as-is
  - Prepends Cursor .mdc frontmatter with `alwaysApply: true`, `description`, `globs: ["**/*"]`
- Modified install loop to branch on `t === 'cursor'`:
  - Uses `generateCursorMdc(content)` for file content instead of raw SKILL.md
  - Prints "Installed (Cursor .mdc): .cursor/rules/mayar.mdc" label for cursor target
  - All other targets continue using raw SKILL.md content
- Force/skip logic unchanged — works identically for all targets including cursor
- Verified output: exactly 2 `^---$` YAML delimiters (no nesting), Cursor frontmatter keys present, original SKILL.md frontmatter keys absent, body content intact
- All 70 existing tests pass — no regressions

### Key Decisions
- `generateCursorMdc` is a module-private function (not exported) — only used internally by the install loop
- YAML frontmatter stripping uses `lines[i].trim() === '---'` to match delimiter lines — `trim()` handles potential trailing whitespace/carriage returns
- Frontmatter is always trimmed from body, then body is re-trimmed at the end — ensures clean output without leading/trailing whitespace
- The `--target agents` case naturally excludes cursor since the targets array is `['agents']` only

### What Next
- task-007: Register `skill` command in cli.js handlers map + update HELP text

---

## Session 2026-07-20 — task-007: Register skill command in cli.js + update HELP

### Completed
- Added `skill: './commands/skill'` to the handlers map in `src/cli.js`
- Added "Agent Skills" section to HELP text showing `skill install` usage with `--target` and `--force` flags
- Verified Global flags section already includes `--sandbox`, `--production`, `--env` (added in task-002)
- Verified Environment section already shows correct resolution order (added in task-002)
- All 70 existing tests pass — no regressions
- Smoke-verified all acceptance criteria: `mayar skill install` runs, `mayar -h` shows new section, unknown `skillx` errors, skill routing works

### Key Decisions
- Agent Skills section placed right after Setup, before Account — matches the logical grouping of setup-time commands
- No change to `--env` HELP text format (uses `<value>` not `<sandbox|production>`, already functionally clear)
- Did not modify `ensureKey()` behavior — skill command now requires API key authentication via the same guard as all other commands in the handlers map

### What Next
- Feature complete — all 7 tasks done
